methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Mission Barns, a Series B biotech company founded by Eitan Fischer and David Bowman, has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis employs two applicable methods within a peer universe of 5 private Series B-stage biotech companies and 3 public sector comparables.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $50M raised proxy, implying a $95M valuation. The Peer-Median approach anchors to a cohort of five same-stage, same-sector companies (Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, Neko Health at $1.8B, New Limit at $810M, Asimov at $800M, and Profluent at $600M), yielding a median of $810M with a range of $600M–$3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied as Mission Barns's revenue is not disclosed. Three public biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) show a sector P/S median of 6.1x, but lack of target revenue prevents triangulation.

The two applicable methods imply a valuation range of $81M–$931M, with a median of $452M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion within the peer set—particularly Eikon and Neko Health at the high end versus Asimov and Profluent at the low end. Key uncertainties include Mission Barns's revenue and path to profitability relative to peers, which would sharpen valuation bounds and help position the company within its peer distribution.
